testcase.name: Level Private State Provider > 'get' returns null if key does not exist
Level Private State Provider > 'getSigningKey' returns null if the signing key does not exist
Level Private State Provider > 'get' functions do not interfere
Level Private State Provider > LevelDB PrivateStateProvider 'get' then 'set' returns original value > for booleans
Level Private State Provider > LevelDB PrivateStateProvider 'get' then 'set' returns original value > for boolean arrays
Level Private State Provider > LevelDB PrivateStateProvider 'get' then 'set' returns original value > for strings
Level Private State Provider > LevelDB PrivateStateProvider 'get' then 'set' returns original value > for string arrays
Level Private State Provider > LevelDB PrivateStateProvider 'get' then 'set' returns original value > for numbers
Level Private State Provider > LevelDB PrivateStateProvider 'get' then 'set' returns original value > for number arrays
Level Private State Provider > LevelDB PrivateStateProvider 'get' then 'set' returns original value > for objects
Level Private State Provider > LevelDB PrivateStateProvider 'get' then 'set' returns original value > for object arrays
Level Private State Provider > LevelDB PrivateStateProvider 'get' then 'set' returns original value > for Uint8 arrays
Level Private State Provider > LevelDB PrivateStateProvider 'get' then 'set' returns original value > for Uint8 array arrays
Level Private State Provider > LevelDB PrivateStateProvider 'get' then 'set' returns original value > for buffers
Level Private State Provider > LevelDB PrivateStateProvider 'get' then 'set' returns original value > for buffer arrays
Level Private State Provider > LevelDB PrivateStateProvider 'get' then 'set' returns original value > for signing keys
Level Private State Provider > 'set' functions do not interfere
Level Private State Provider > 'remove' deletes private states
Level Private State Provider > 'removeSigningKey' deletes signing keys
Level Private State Provider > 'remove' functions do not interfere
Level Private State Provider > 'clear' deletes private states
Level Private State Provider > 'clearSigningKeys' deletes signing keys
Level Private State Provider > 'clear' functions do not interfere
Level Private State Provider > 'get' returns null for non-existent scoped key
Level Private State Provider > 'getSigningKey' throws error on non-'LEVEL_NOT_FOUND_ERROR' codes
Level Private State Provider > Password provider configuration > throws error when privateStoragePasswordProvider is not provided
Level Private State Provider > Password provider configuration > works correctly when privateStoragePasswordProvider is provided
Level Private State Provider > Contract address scoping > throws error when get is called without setContractAddress
Level Private State Provider > Contract address scoping > throws error when set is called without setContractAddress
Level Private State Provider > Contract address scoping > throws error when remove is called without setContractAddress
Level Private State Provider > Contract address scoping > throws error when clear is called without setContractAddress
Level Private State Provider > Contract address scoping > allows operations after setContractAddress is called
Level Private State Provider > Contract address scoping > provides namespace isolation between different contract addresses
Level Private State Provider > Contract address scoping > same stateId with different contract addresses are independent
Level Private State Provider > Contract address scoping > remove only affects the current contract address scope
Level Private State Provider > Contract address scoping > signing key operations do not require setContractAddress
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > exports and imports private states correctly
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > exports with custom password and imports with same password
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > throws ExportDecryptionError on wrong password
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > throws PrivateStateExportError when no states to export
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > throws PrivateStateExportError for short custom password
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > throws PrivateStateExportError for short import password
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > throws ImportConflictError when conflict strategy is error (default)
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > ImportConflictError contains count but not state IDs
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > skips conflicts when strategy is skip
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > overwrites conflicts when strategy is overwrite
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > throws InvalidExportFormatError for wrong format identifier
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > throws InvalidExportFormatError for missing fields
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > throws InvalidExportFormatError for invalid salt length
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > throws InvalidExportFormatError for invalid salt characters
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > does NOT export signing keys
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > preserves complex types (Buffer, Uint8Array) through export/import
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > handles mixed import scenarios correctly
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > enforces maxStates limit on export
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > enforces maxStates limit on import
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > malformed data edge cases > throws ExportDecryptionError for garbage base64 payload
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > malformed data edge cases > throws ExportDecryptionError for payload that decrypts to invalid JSON
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > malformed data edge cases > throws ExportDecryptionError for payload with invalid structure (missing states)
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > malformed data edge cases > throws ExportDecryptionError for payload with invalid structure (missing version)
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > malformed data edge cases > throws ExportDecryptionError for stateCount mismatch
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > malformed data edge cases > throws InvalidExportFormatError for unsupported version
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > malformed data edge cases > throws error for state values that fail superjson.parse
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > malformed data edge cases > throws ExportDecryptionError for tampered encrypted payload
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > malformed data edge cases > throws InvalidExportFormatError for empty salt
Level Private State Provider > Export/Import > malformed data edge cases > throws InvalidExportFormatError for salt with uppercase hex (validates exact format)
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > exports and imports signing keys correctly
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > exports with custom password and imports with same password
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > throws ExportDecryptionError on wrong password
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > throws SigningKeyExportError when no keys to export
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > throws SigningKeyExportError for short custom password
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > throws SigningKeyExportError for short import password
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > throws ImportConflictError when conflict strategy is error (default)
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > ImportConflictError contains count but not addresses
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > skips conflicts when strategy is skip
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > overwrites conflicts when strategy is overwrite
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > throws InvalidExportFormatError for wrong format identifier
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > throws InvalidExportFormatError for missing fields
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > throws InvalidExportFormatError for invalid salt length
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > throws InvalidExportFormatError for invalid salt characters
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > enforces maxKeys limit on export
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > enforces maxKeys limit on import
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > handles mixed import scenarios correctly
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > malformed data edge cases > throws ExportDecryptionError for garbage base64 payload
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > malformed data edge cases > throws ExportDecryptionError for payload that decrypts to invalid JSON
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > malformed data edge cases > throws ExportDecryptionError for payload with invalid structure (missing keys)
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > malformed data edge cases > throws ExportDecryptionError for payload with invalid structure (missing version)
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > malformed data edge cases > throws ExportDecryptionError for keyCount mismatch
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > malformed data edge cases > throws InvalidExportFormatError for unsupported version
Level Private State Provider > Signing Key Export/Import > malformed data edge cases > throws ExportDecryptionError for tampered encrypted payload
Level Private State Provider > Browser Warning > shows warning in browser environment
Level Private State Provider > Browser Warning > does not show warning in Node.js environment
Level Private State Provider > Browser Warning > does not show warning when only window exists (e.g., jsdom)
Level Private State Provider > Browser Warning > shows warning only once per session
Level Private State Provider > Browser Warning > handles sessionStorage errors gracefully
testcase.system-out: MIDNIGHT: Could not access sessionStorage for warning deduplication: Storage error

testcase.name: Level Private State Provider > Salt Consistency (Race Condition Prevention) > multiple sequential operations maintain consistent salt for private states
Level Private State Provider > Salt Consistency (Race Condition Prevention) > multiple sequential operations maintain consistent salt for signing keys
Level Private State Provider > Salt Consistency (Race Condition Prevention) > operations after clear() create new consistent salt
Level Private State Provider > Salt Consistency (Race Condition Prevention) > mixed operations on private states and signing keys use separate consistent salts
Level Private State Provider > Salt Consistency (Race Condition Prevention) > operations after clearSigningKeys() create new consistent salt for signing keys
Level Private State Provider > Encryption Cache > invalidateEncryptionCache method exists on provider
Level Private State Provider > Encryption Cache > encryption instance is reused for multiple operations (caching works)
Level Private State Provider > Encryption Cache > cache is invalidated when password changes
Level Private State Provider > Encryption Cache > invalidateEncryptionCache clears cache and forces re-derivation
Level Private State Provider > Encryption Cache > private states and signing keys have separate cache entries
Level Private State Provider > Encryption Cache > cache survives multiple provider instances with same config
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > changePassword > changePassword method exists on provider
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > changePassword > throws error when contract address not set
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > changePassword > successfully rotates password for private states
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > changePassword > throws error when old password is incorrect
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > changePassword > handles empty database gracefully
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > changePassword > re-encrypts all contracts data in sublevel to prevent data loss
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > changePassword > invalidates encryption cache after rotation
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > changePassword > validates new password meets requirements
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > changePassword > migrates V1-encrypted data to V2 during rotation
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > changeSigningKeysPassword > changeSigningKeysPassword method exists on provider
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > changeSigningKeysPassword > successfully rotates password for signing keys
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > changeSigningKeysPassword > throws error when old password is incorrect
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > changeSigningKeysPassword > handles empty signing keys gracefully
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > changeSigningKeysPassword > does not require contract address to be set
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > changeSigningKeysPassword > invalidates encryption cache after rotation
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > Rotation Result > changePassword returns entriesMigrated count
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > Rotation Result > changePassword returns zero for empty database
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > Rotation Result > changeSigningKeysPassword returns entriesMigrated count
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > Concurrent Access > concurrent rotations are serialized correctly
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > Concurrent Access > remove waits for rotation lock before executing
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > Concurrent Access > removeSigningKey waits for rotation lock before executing
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > Concurrent Access > get waits for rotation lock before executing
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > Concurrent Access > set waits for rotation lock before executing
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > Concurrent Access > getSigningKey waits for rotation lock before executing
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > Concurrent Access > setSigningKey waits for rotation lock before executing
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > Memory Limit > throws error when entry count exceeds maxEntries option
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > Memory Limit > changeSigningKeysPassword throws error when entry count exceeds limit
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > Early Password Validation > fails fast with incorrect old password before iterating all entries
Level Private State Provider > Password Rotation > Early Password Validation > changeSigningKeysPassword fails fast with incorrect old password
Level Private State Provider > Account Isolation > different accountIds have completely isolated private state storage
Level Private State Provider > Account Isolation > different accountIds have completely isolated signing key storage
Level Private State Provider > Account Isolation > account 2 cannot read data from account 1
Level Private State Provider > Account Isolation > throws error for whitespace-only accountId
Level Private State Provider > Account Isolation > clear() only affects data for the specific account
Level Private State Provider > Account Isolation > clearSigningKeys() only affects data for the specific account
Level Private State Provider > Account Isolation > same accountId produces same scoped storage path consistently
Level Private State Provider > migrateToAccountScoped > migrates private states from unscoped to scoped sublevel
Level Private State Provider > migrateToAccountScoped > migrates signing keys from unscoped to scoped sublevel
Level Private State Provider > migrateToAccountScoped > returns zero counts when no data to migrate
Level Private State Provider > migrateToAccountScoped > throws error for missing accountId
Level Private State Provider > migrateToAccountScoped > throws error for whitespace-only accountId
Level Private State Provider > migrateToAccountScoped > migrated data is accessible via scoped provider
Level Private State Provider > migrateToAccountScoped > migration is idempotent - running twice does not corrupt data
Level Private State Provider > migrateToAccountScoped > preserves original unscoped data after migration
StorageEncryption > encrypt and decrypt > successfully encrypts and decrypts data
StorageEncryption > encrypt and decrypt > produces different ciphertext for same plaintext
StorageEncryption > encrypt and decrypt > handles empty string
StorageEncryption > encrypt and decrypt > handles unicode characters
StorageEncryption > error handling > throws on wrong password
StorageEncryption > version migration > decrypts v1 encrypted data with 100k iterations using decryptWithPassword
StorageEncryption > version migration > new encryption uses version 2
StorageEncryption > version migration > v2 encrypted data can be decrypted
StorageEncryption > version migration > detects encryption version correctly
StorageEncryption > version migration > decrypt throws when V1 data is encountered without password
StorageEncryption > version migration > decryptWithPassword works for V2 data as well
StorageEncryption > password verification > verifyPassword returns true for correct password
StorageEncryption > password verification > verifyPassword returns false for incorrect password
StorageEncryption > password verification > verifyPassword is case-sensitive
StorageEncryption > password verification > password is not stored in plaintext
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > throws error when password provider returns empty string
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > throws error when password is too short
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > returns password when valid
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > works with async provider
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > character class requirements > rejects password with only lowercase letters
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > character class requirements > rejects password with only uppercase letters
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > character class requirements > rejects password with only digits
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > character class requirements > rejects password with only two character classes (lowercase + digits)
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > character class requirements > accepts password with three character classes (lowercase + uppercase + digits)
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > character class requirements > accepts password with three character classes (lowercase + uppercase + special)
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > character class requirements > accepts password with all four character classes
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > repeated character requirements > rejects password with more than 3 consecutive identical characters
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > repeated character requirements > rejects password of all identical characters
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > repeated character requirements > accepts password with exactly 3 consecutive identical characters
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > sequential pattern requirements > rejects password with ascending numeric sequence
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > sequential pattern requirements > rejects password with descending numeric sequence
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > sequential pattern requirements > rejects password with ascending letter sequence
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > sequential pattern requirements > rejects password with descending letter sequence
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > sequential pattern requirements > accepts password with non-sequential characters
StorageEncryption > getPasswordFromProvider > sequential pattern requirements > accepts password with short sequences (3 chars is ok)
